Scutellaria chinensis, commonly known as Huang Qin, is a plant species belonging to the Lamiaceae family, traditionally used in Chinese medicine. The plant is valued for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which have been shown to support the treatment of respiratory and gastrointestinal issues. The bioactive constituents of Scutellaria chinensis include flavonoids, such as baicalein, baicalin, and wogonin, as well as phenolic acids like ferulic acid and caffeic acid. In traditional Chinese medicine, Scutellaria chinensis is commonly used to prepare teas, capsules, and infusions, as well as being incorporated into herbal soups and decoctions.
